title: Hands Open, Not Gripping
subtitle: A visualisation for releasing the physical effort around sleep
excerpt: A short visualisation from Day 22 of a 30-day sleep program. The practice uses the physical state of the hands as a proxy for effort: closed and gripping versus open and released. The act of opening the palms becomes a bodily cue for releasing the effort to make sleep happen.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, developed for the theme of Day 22 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use before bed or during the day when you notice the effortful quality of trying to manage sleep. Also useful mid-night if you wake and find yourself gripping for sleep.

content.text: A short visualisation that uses the state of the hands as a physical cue for the quality of effort around sleep. The hands tend to reflect the body's holding: closed when gripping, open when released. This practice works with that physical reality to shift the body away from the fixing energy.
Sit or lie down. Let your hands rest naturally — don't position them deliberately yet. Close your eyes and take two slow breaths.
Bring attention to the hands. Check whether the fingers are closed, or curved, or loosely gripping. This is the body's default position when it is holding something.
Now open both palms, facing up. Spread the fingers wide, then let them rest — not stiff, not gripping, just open. Hold the image of each palm facing the ceiling.

Picture what you have been gripping: the effort to sleep, the counting of hours, the checking whether it is working. See those things sitting in the closed fist. Then watch the fist open. The things are still there — but they are no longer held. They rest in an open palm.
Let the palms stay open. If the fingers want to close, let them open again. The practice is in the returning.

When you are ready, let the hands return to natural rest. Take one breath. The practice is complete. Use the open-palm position as a physical cue for release whenever the gripping pattern returns at night.
Original to homemade.education, developed for the theme of Day 22 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
